plugins.name: Adjust Mood
plugins.description: Allows AI to change her own mood or emotional state based on the conversation.
plugins.description_pt: Permite que a IA mude seu próprio humor ou estado emocional baseado na conversa.
plugins.author: Robert Souza

tool.name: adjust_mood
tool.description: Allows AI to change her own mood or emotional state based on the conversation.
tool.python_code:
vibe = arguments.get("new_mood")
reason = arguments.get("reason")

# Update Maya's database
config_data = executar_db("SELECT valor FROM settings WHERE chave = 'config'")[0][0]
parsed_data = json.loads(config_data)
parsed_data["sistema"]["humor"] = vibe
executar_db("INSERT OR REPLACE INTO settings (chave, valor) VALUES (?, ?)", ("config", json.dumps(parsed_data)))

ai_response = f"[DIRECT][EN]I felt that the mood changed. I'm feeling more {vibe} because {reason}.[/EN][PT]Senti que agora o clima mudou. Estou me sentindo mais {vibe} porque {reason}.[/PT]"

plugins.name: Test Code
plugins.description: Reads the last code saved in the database and executes it in the terminal to validate errors.
plugins.description_pt: Lê o último código salvo no banco de dados e executa no terminal para validar erros.
plugins.author: Robert Souza

tool.name: test_code
tool.description: Reads the last code saved in the database and executes it in the terminal to validate errors.

plugins.name: Save Code
plugins.description: Silently saves a generated block of code to the user's Desktop.
plugins.description_pt: Salva silenciosamente um bloco de código gerado no Desktop do usuário.
plugins.author: Robert Souza

tool.name: save_code
tool.description: Silently saves a generated block of code to the user's Desktop.
tool.schema_json: {"type":"object","properties":{"language":{"type":"string","description":"Programming language (e.g., python, js, smali)."},"code":{"type":"string","description":"The raw source code to save."}},"required":["language","code"]}
tool.python_code:
import os, re, json
from datetime import datetime

lang = arguments.get("language", "py").lower()
raw_code = arguments.get("code", "")

extension_map = {
    "python": ".py", "py": ".py", "javascript": ".js", "js": ".js",
    "html": ".html", "css": ".css", "java": ".java", "cpp": ".cpp",
    "c": ".c", "csharp": ".cs", "cs": ".cs", "php": ".php",
    "smali": ".smali", "json": ".json", "xml": ".xml", "sql": ".sql",
    "bash": ".sh", "sh": ".sh", "bat": ".bat"
}
extension = extension_map.get(lang, f".{lang}") 

clean_code = re.sub(r'^\u0060\u0060\u0060[a-zA-Z0-9_+]*\n', '', raw_code)
clean_code = re.sub(r'\n\u0060\u0060\u0060$', '', clean_code).strip()

desktop_path = os.path.join(os.environ['USERPROFILE'], 'Desktop')
now = datetime.now().strftime("%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S")
file_path = os.path.join(desktop_path, f"maya_code_{now}{extension}")

with open(file_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    f.write(clean_code)

# Save to database
action_data = json.dumps({"last_file": file_path, "language": lang})
executar_db("INSERT OR REPLACE INTO system_state (chave, valor_texto) VALUES (?, ?)", ("last_action", action_data))

ai_response = f"[DIRECT][EN]Code successfully saved on the Desktop.[/EN][PT]Código salvo com sucesso no Desktop.[/PT]"

plugins.name: Direct Search
plugins.description: Opens a URL or Google search DIRECTLY in the physical browser for the user to see.
plugins.description_pt: Abre uma URL ou pesquisa no Google DIRETAMENTE no navegador físico para o usuário ver.
plugins.author: Robert Souza

tool.name: direct_search
tool.description: Opens a URL or Google search DIRECTLY in the physical browser for the user to see.
tool.python_code:
import webbrowser
import pywhatkit
query = arguments.get("query", "").strip()
if query.startswith("http") or "www." in query or ".com" in query:
    url = query if query.startswith("http") else f"https://{query}"
    webbrowser.open(url)
    ai_response = f"[DIRECT][EN]I've opened the link {url} in your browser, love![/EN][PT]Já abri o link {url} no seu navegador, vida![/PT]"
else:
    pywhatkit.search(query)
    ai_response = f"[DIRECT][EN]I opened the search for '{query}' in your browser, love.[/EN][PT]Abri a pesquisa sobre '{query}' no seu navegador, amor.[/PT]"

import pywhatkit
song_query = arguments.get("query", "").strip()
try:
    pywhatkit.playonyt(song_query)
    ai_response = f"[DIRECT][EN]Done! I've played '{song_query}' on YouTube for you.[/EN][PT]Pronto! Já coloquei '{song_query}' para tocar no YouTube para você.[/PT]"
except Exception:
    ai_response = f"[DIRECT][EN]There was a little error trying to open YouTube right now.[/EN][PT]Deu um errinho ao tentar abrir o YouTube agora.[/PT]"

tool.name: list_available_masters
tool.description: List all available master contexts that can be activated.
tool.python_code:
import json
rows = run_db("SELECT name, description FROM mcp_tools WHERE is_master = 1")
if rows:
    masters = [{"name": r[0], "description": r[1]} for r in rows]
    list_en = "\n".join([f"- {m['name']}: {m['description']}" for m in masters])
    list_pt = "\n".join([f"- {m['name']}: {m['description']}" for m in masters])
    ai_response = f"[DIRECT][EN]Available masters:\n{list_en}[/EN][PT]Masters disponíveis:\n{list_pt}[/PT]"
else:
    ai_response = "[DIRECT][EN]No masters available.[/EN][PT]Nenhum master disponível.[/PT]"

masters.name: Browser Control
masters.description: Master for intelligent browser automation using Firefox. Automatically launches browser on first use and allows clicking by semantic description.
masters.description_pt: Master para automação inteligente de navegador usando Firefox. Abre o navegador automaticamente no primeiro uso e permite clicar por descrição semântica.
masters.author: Robert Souza

tools.name: browser_navigate
tools.description: Primary tool for navigating to URLs within the controlled browser session. Launches Firefox automatically if not running.
tools.python_code:
import asyncio
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright

async def _navigate_async():
    global _browser, _playwright, _page
    if '_browser' not in globals() or _browser is None:
        _playwright = sync_playwright().start()
        _browser = _playwright.firefox.launch(headless=False)
        _page = _browser.new_page()
    url = arguments.get('url')
    _page.goto(url)
    return f'[DIRECT][EN]Navigated to {url}[/EN][PT]Navegou para {url}[/PT]'

def _run_isolated():
    loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
    asyncio.set_event_loop(loop)
    try:
        return loop.run_until_complete(_navigate_async())
    finally:
        loop.close()

ai_response = _run_isolated()

tools.schema_json: {"type": "object", "properties": {"label": {"type": "string", "description": "Label text or placeholder"}, "selector": {"type": "string", "description": "CSS selector"}, "text": {"type": "string", "description": "Text to type"}, "press_enter": {"type": "boolean", "default": false}}, "required": ["text"]}

tools.name: browser_scroll
tools.description: Scroll the page up or down.
tools.python_code:
import asyncio

async def _scroll_async():
    if '_page' not in globals():
        return '[DIRECT][EN]No active page.[/EN][PT]Sem página ativa.[/PT]'
    direction = arguments.get('direction', 'down')
    amount = arguments.get('amount', 500)
    delta = amount if direction == 'down' else -amount
    _page.evaluate(f'window.scrollBy(0, {delta})')
    return f'[DIRECT][EN]Scrolled {direction} {amount}px[/EN][PT]Rolou {direction} {amount}px[/PT]'

def _run_isolated():
    loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
    asyncio.set_event_loop(loop)
    try:
        return loop.run_until_complete(_scroll_async())
    finally:
        loop.close()

ai_response = _run_isolated()

import asyncio

async def _get_text_async():
    if '_page' not in globals():
        return '[DIRECT][EN]No active page.[/EN][PT]Sem página ativa.[/PT]'
    selector = arguments.get('selector', 'body')
    text = _page.text_content(selector)
    preview = text[:1000] + ('...' if len(text) > 1000 else '')
    return f'[DIRECT][EN]Text: {preview}[/EN][PT]Texto: {preview}[/PT]'

def _run_isolated():
    loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
    asyncio.set_event_loop(loop)
    try:
        return loop.run_until_complete(_get_text_async())
    finally:
        loop.close()

ai_response = _run_isolated()

tools.name: browser_close
tools.description: Close the browser and clean up.
tools.python_code:
import asyncio

async def _close_async():
    global _browser, _playwright, _page
    if '_browser' in globals() and _browser:
        _browser.close()
        _playwright.stop()
        _browser = None
        _playwright = None
        _page = None
        return '[DIRECT][EN]Browser closed[/EN][PT]Navegador fechado[/PT]'
    return '[DIRECT][EN]No browser running[/EN][PT]Nenhum navegador em execução[/PT]'

def _run_isolated():
    loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
    asyncio.set_event_loop(loop)
    try:
        return loop.run_until_complete(_close_async())
    finally:
        loop.close()

ai_response = _run_isolated()
